Correct answer: D
Solution

behaviour due to unsequenced modifications.

Q7 MCQ Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following is true about linked list implementation of queue?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
To keep the first in first out order a queue can be implemented using linked list in 2 ways.
1. If a new node is inserted at the beginning of linked list, then in dequeue operation node must be removed from end.
2. If new node is inserted at the end of linked list then, in dequeue operation, node must be
removed from beginning.

Q14 MCQ Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The following C function takes a singly linked list as input argument. It prints its elements from the end using with the help of some other data structure. Some part of the code is left blank.

 
Choose the correct alternative to replace the blank line.
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Applying recursion, traverse the linked list till the last element, hence at every step, the element of the linked list will be stored in the stack. While coming back, print the elements, hence the elements will be start printing from the end.
if (!head) return;
printlist (head → next);
printf(“%d”, head → data);

Q18 MCQ Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following C program segment:

 
What is the output of above C program when it runs on a root node of a binary tree? (Assume level numbering starts from 1 (the root is at level 1)
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The above program segment print the node value when it is at distance ‘K’ from root node otherwise it return ‘0’, If length of binary tree from root node is less than ‘K’ then program return ‘0’.
Only when height is greater than ‘K’ the program prints the node value of distance ‘K’ from root node.

Q22 MCQ Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following defined in C program:
int **fun (int**);
What does the above definition means:

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• int indicates an integer variable.
• int * indicates a pointer to an integer variable.
• int ** indicate a pointer to pointer to an integer variable.

II. In AVL, the time taken to find a non-existent element ‘x’ in the best case is Ο(log n).
III. In binary tree, the time taken to find a non-existent element ‘x’ in the best case is Ο(n).
How many number of statements is correct?

NAT
Correct answer: 3
Solution

3

 
Q27 MCQ Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Suppose that queue operations are implemented using stack operation. Enqueue (x) and Dequeue
( ) are queue operations whereas Pop( ) and Push(x) are stack operations.
[Note: Consider two states S1 and S2]
Consider the following pseudocode:

 
 
What are the messing values of B1 and B2 to perform Dequeue operation correctly where S1 and S2 are two stacks.
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
If S2 stack is empty and S1 stack is not empty then we have to pop the element from stack S2 and
push that element into stack S2 and return the stack S2 which contain newly inserted element.

Q32 MCQ Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following C program:

 
Which of the following is correct regarding above program?
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
After “free(p);” line, pointer p become dangling pointer. Because now p is pointing to deallocated memory.
In ‘if’ condition, the value is –1 and it is not zero, so if condition is true and program will execute
statements inside ‘if’. Here malloc( ) function allocate new memory to pointer p and program will
lost the old allocated memory to p. So, it is memory leak.
